criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose lateral ischemia, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of ST-segment depression in leads II, III, and aVF
Presence of ST-segment depression in lateral leads
Presence of diffuse concave ST-segment elevation in lateral leads
Presence of J-point elevation in lateral leads
Presence of inverted T waves in anterior leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of ST-segment depression in lateral leads

finding.question: Reagrding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show ST depression in at least two of the lateral leads, including leads I, aVL, V5, and V6?

grounding.question: Which of the following leads show ST depression? Select all possible leads from the options below.
Within the selected leads, in which of the following segments can you observe the QRS complexes with ST depression? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

decision.question: based on the finding identified above, does this patient have lateral ischemia?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
